Step #3: Your USP
As I explain in the video, I quickly learned that the "classic" definition wasn't doing much for the average
entrepreneur or small business owner who just wanted to find a good position in their market to make
some Big Bucks.
So I futzed with the "theory", and tested it in the real world. For decades.
What I use is very different than the "classic" USP. However, my version is actually more user-friendly when
you sit down to figure out where you stand in your market.
Without this basic understanding, you're flying blind with your marketing.
Once you go through the exercise, however… all the mystery of your "position in the niche" will vanish…
… and you can get busy either exploiting where you're coming from…
… or move to a new position where the profit and fun may be more plentiful.
It all hinges on getting away from B.S. theory… and actually applying real-world, workable insight to your
marketing.
In this section, I also reveal how easy it is to use the pro-level trick of putting together your own "elevator
chat"… so you can communicate to anyone, quickly and super-efficiently, exactly what you're offering, and
why it's something they should look into.
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 1 of 22 --

Your USP
Position yourself…
uniquely in your market…
in order to sell.
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 22 --

Your Elevator Chat
For yourself, or your client… when meeting
a perfect prospect:
• Who ARE you?
• What do you do or sell?
• How long have you been in this biz (or how
successful have you been)?
• How do you help your customers?
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 22 --

Your USP
• Company Line (why clients “believe”
people buy) vs. Sales Detective Research
(why people actually buy…
• Boring ( the company line) vs. Sexy
(direct hit)…
• It ain’t about you at all…
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 22 --

Your USP
● Not “what” you sell…
● But what “what” you sell DOES for your
customer.
● Aim for the “Id ticklers”: Faster, easier,
simpler, a better bargain, an unfair
advantage, inside information, privilege
and luxury, bragging rights.
● Remember: You will need to PROVE
everything immediately. To a VERY
skeptical reader.
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 22 --

Your USP
• Think in terms of a non-clever slogan.
• Do not deepen the mystery – solve it with
specifics.
• “Starter” formula:“We help [this group of
people]… do [this benefit(s)]… [better].”
• Positioning addition:“… [better] than
[competitor or common wisdom].”
• Benefit addition “...even if [worst situation
possible].”
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 22 --

Your USP
We help [this group of people]… do [this
benefit(s)]… [better] than [competitor or
common wisdom]…
...even if [worst situation possible]
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 22 --

WARNING
Do not fall in love with your USP.
It will not sell on its own. It is merely a
way to SET UP interest and get your
foot in the door.
It is, however, also the foundation of
your best headlines.
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 21 of 22 --

Step #3: Your USP
Step #3 Exercise:
Now, it's your turn to sit down and create your USP as John explained in the video.
Position yourself…uniquely in your market…in order to sell.
Company Line (vague bullshit) vs. Sales Detective Research (why people actually buy)…
Boring vs. Sexy…
It ain’t about you at all…
Not “what” you sell… But what “what” you sell DOES for your customer.
Aim for the “Id ticklers”: Faster, easier, simpler, a better bargain, an unfair advantage,
inside information, privilege and luxury, bragging rights.
Remember: You will need to PROVE everything immediately. To a VERY skeptical reader.
Think in terms of a non-clever slogan. Do not deepen the mystery – solve it with
specifics.
“Starter” formula: “We help [this group of people]…do [this benefit(s)]…[better].”
Positioning addition: “…[better] than [competitor or common wisdom].”
Benefit addition: “… even if [worst situation possible].”
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 22 of 22 --
